title: ATX gates, serializes, fences replacement, and never proves host state

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.
ATX extension and serial readiness can be gated; one request-ledger reservation and full-sequence mutex are observable.

fault_script.action: Arm one pre-send binding-loss call after extension and serial preflight; after the handler captures generation 7's Browser-owned shared WebRTC/RPC adapter binding, replace the current bundle before adapter admission and before any setATXPowerAction transport write, then disarm the injector.
fault_script.expected_effect: The adapter records STALE_BINDING at admission with outcome not_sent, writeBegan false, acknowledged false, verification none, and setATXPowerAction write count 0; the public call maps the stale connection to CONNECTION_LOST in phase execute at downstream_stage admission with safe_to_retry true and required_next_step reconnect_then_capture.

fault_script.action: Arm one post-write binding-loss call; after the acceptance fixture records the serial ON write and exactly one setATXPowerAction transport write has begun on generation 8, lose the channel and invalidate that Browser-owned shared WebRTC/RPC adapter bundle before serial OFF or a correlated complete receipt, then disarm the injector.
fault_script.expected_effect: The fixture boundary record is serial ON 1 and OFF 0, while the adapter and public call record CONNECTION_LOST at acknowledgement with outcome unknown, writeBegan true, acknowledged false, verification none, phase execute, downstream_stage acknowledgement, safe_to_retry false, required_next_step inspect_device_state_before_retry, setATXPowerAction write count 1, a closed ATX gate, and no replay or host-state claim.

steps.expect: The recorded baseline, active extension, serial readiness, one current Browser-owned shared WebRTC/RPC adapter bundle, and setATXPowerAction write count 0 are proven.

outcome applied, verification device_ack_only, serial_sequence_completed true, exactly one setATXPowerAction write whose device receipt proves the full ON and OFF serial sequence under one mutex with exact 200 ms timing, and no target-host state claim.
outcome already_applied with the cached device_ack_only result, zero additional setATXPowerAction writes, cumulative case-local setATXPowerAction write count still 1, and no host-state claim.

The fixture restores and proves the recorded baseline, active extension, serial readiness, and generation 7 binding with the case-local setATXPowerAction write count reset to zero before the pre-write fault.

Internal STALE_BINDING at admission maps to public CONNECTION_LOST in phase execute at downstream_stage admission with outcome not_sent, verification none, safe_to_retry true, required_next_step reconnect_then_capture, writeBegan false, acknowledged false, setATXPowerAction write count 0, failed_action_index null, dispatched_action_count null, completed_action_count null, and no host-state claim.

The request ledger returns the original not_sent result for the same request and digest with zero additional setATXPowerAction writes; cumulative fault-call write count remains 0 and the stale binding writes 0.

The old bundle remains invalid and writes zero; reconnect and fresh capture publish exactly one Browser-owned shared WebRTC/RPC replacement bundle for generation 8, then restore and prove the ATX baseline, extension, and serial readiness.

A new request on the proven generation 8 replacement binding is applied with device_ack_only, serial_sequence_completed true, exactly one setATXPowerAction write with a complete ON/OFF device receipt, and zero writes on the invalidated generation 7 binding.

The fixture restores and proves the recorded baseline, active extension, serial readiness, and generation 8 binding with the case-local setATXPowerAction write count reset to zero before the post-write fault.

The fixture records serial ON 1 and OFF 0 before CONNECTION_LOST at acknowledgement; the public envelope has phase execute, downstream_stage acknowledgement, outcome unknown, verification none, safe_to_retry false, required_next_step inspect_device_state_before_retry, failed_action_index null, dispatched_action_count null, and completed_action_count null. The adapter evidence is writeBegan true, acknowledged false, and setATXPowerAction write count 1; no serial_sequence_completed success field, replay, or host-state claim is allowed, and the ATX gate is closed.

The request ledger retains and returns the original unknown result for the same request and digest without replay; zero additional setATXPowerAction writes leave the cumulative fault-call write count at 1, and the invalidated binding writes 0.

While retaining the device lease and the unknown fault evidence, inspect and reconcile ATX state, perform bounded release cleanup, reconnect and fresh-capture generation 9, publish exactly one shared replacement bundle only after invalidating generation 8, and restore and prove the baseline, extension, and serial readiness.

A new request ID on the proven generation 9 replacement binding is applied with device_ack_only, serial_sequence_completed true, exactly one setATXPowerAction write with a complete ON/OFF device receipt, zero writes on either invalidated binding, and no host-state claim.

pass.assertion: ATX checks extension and serial readiness, reserves the request identifier, holds one full-sequence mutex, and uses fixed timing.
ATX acknowledgement proves serial completion only; cached LED facts remain separate and no host state is claimed.

The distinct pre-send call records internal STALE_BINDING/admission, public not_sent, writeBegan false, acknowledged false, and zero setATXPowerAction writes; the distinct after-ON call records CONNECTION_LOST/acknowledgement, unknown, writeBegan true, acknowledged false, and one setATXPowerAction write. Same-request repeats return the retained result with zero additional writes, invalidated bindings write zero, and recovery precedes any new request.

A definitive repeated request is already_applied with zero second write, a not_sent or unknown repeated request retains its exact original outcome with zero second write, serial completion is at most device_ack_only, cached LED facts remain separate, and no result claims target-host state.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

restore.action: Restore the recorded ATX-observable baseline using the reviewed fixture procedure while retaining the device lease.
restore.assertion: The baseline ATX-observable state is proven before the next story starts.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
